memory_recovered: Continuity identifiers are a repository-wide namespace across the sanctioned index, records, and memory roots; directory-local uniqueness is insufficient.

contradiction.observed_condition: operations/continuity/records and operations/continuity/memory contain distinct CM-0010 claims while the previous CLI validator scanned only records.
contradiction.preservation_rule: Neither historical CM-0010 file was deleted, renamed, or rewritten.

behavior: rejects a duplicate continuity ID split across records and memory

verification.limitations: No Node or GitHub Actions execution was available in this run.

privacy_boundary: Public repository files and commit metadata only. No private chat text, health material, credentials, identifying personal data, or unreviewed uploads were copied into the artifact.

observed: The prior validator CLI supplied only operations/continuity/records.
Two distinct public repository artifacts use CM-0010 across records and memory.
The revised CLI supplies both sanctioned roots and the test suite contains a cross-root duplicate fixture.
inferred: Future sanctioned roots must be added explicitly or generated from a canonical root registry to avoid another blind spot.
proposed: Add an additive alias or supersession migration record for one CM-0010 and retain a redirect map for external references.
unresolved: Which historical CM-0010 retains the canonical identifier remains undecided.
The repository currently fails the expanded validator until the collision is migrated.

falsification_or_review_route: Run node --test tools/continuity/validate-continuity-records.test.mjs, then run node tools/continuity/validate-continuity-records.mjs at repository root. Reject this evidence if the fixture does not detect a duplicate split across records and memory or if the CLI omits either sanctioned root.
